title: Nahuatl Legacies
editorialSummary: English words borrowed from Nahuatl (via Spanish), grouped by the semantic field through which they entered: food (chocolate, tomato, avocado, chili), animals (ocelot, coyote, axolotl, quetzal), plants/nature (mesquite, copal, zapote, nopal), and objects/culture (hammock is Taino not Nahuatl — excluded; items are: cocoa, atlatl, chipotle, cenote). Every item is a real Nahuatl-origin English word. Hard because players likely know the words but not their origin language, and many feel like they could belong in multiple semantic buckets.
